Issue No.5 • December 2011
Anato~ny Of A Mega Successful
Product Launch Using Nothing
But ''Lowly'' E~nails ...
Dear Subscriber,
Okay, let's get down to bid'niz. This is the last month of 2011 and I want you to go
out with a big loud BANG that can be heard 'round the world (by your customers and
competitors). If there's one thing I've gotten VERY good at (if I do say so mysel~ over the
past couple years it's launching products and holding special sales using email. I've
discussed this in Street-Smart Email from a "bird's eye view." But today I'm going to
swoop down from the skies and show you, email-by-email, how to do it. Do not blatantly
copy the examples, of course (I speaketh of anyone in the weight loss industry reading
this). But use the structure (very important), ideas, themes, etc to your heart's content. I
want you to make a LOT of money dang nab it. And the best way I can help further that
goal is to show you how this works up close and personal. This is going to be a LONG
issue with a lot of examples (and brief analysis of each example). So get settled in and be
prepared to spend some time going through it. I would bet if you applied the info in this
issue to your next launch or sale (this also works great for building a sale) you would see a
tremendous surge of sales. And if you don't have a new product to launch then either
adapt it for a weekend sale or take one of your products down, retool it a bit (you can

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
never over-improve a product) and re-launch it. I have done that many times. Anyway,
let's hit it, maestro ...
This may sound simple, but my entire launch process comes down to 4 steps:
• 	Announce the product
• 	Drive as many people as possible on my main list to a sublist so I have a
count of people who have raised their hand and said, "I'm interested!"
• 	Spend as much time as I can tease, Tease, TEASING both lists (my main
list and sublist) up until launch day
• 	During the launch, email both lists (main list and sublist -- although they
get different emails with different frequencies, as you'll see) multiple times
during a tight period of time to buy, with an "eBay" like countdown on
the last day
Sounds simple, yeah?
Hey, I'm a simple guy. I hate complicated. And every other launch methodology I've
seen involves way too many "moving parts" for my tastes. Not saying they don't work or
that my way is best (it's probably not). But it IS simple. And most people (including clients
I've had) poo-poo it because of the simplicity. And yet, the sales that come in can be just
crazy high if you do it right. So, with that said, I'm going to show you a real life launch
from a few months ago. I am removing the names of the product/author/URL at the
request of my partners. But the ideas arc all there and it won't matter as far as teaching
this is concerned. What is important to note is, this launch made almost twice as many
sales as any of us were expecting. (We had a screw up with the tracking software so I don't
have exact conversions. But needless to say, we were all very happy campers when the
dust settled and the sales counted. In fact one of my partners was reeling happy -- he'd
been involved with many launches for some of the biggest names in the home business
industry and hadn't seen these kinds of results before).
Anyhoo, the first step is (as outlined above) to announce the product. And what you
do is you tell your main list you have a new product in the works (or if you want tore-
launch a product that you're retooling it) and that people who join during the launch

period will get it at a discount (or will get some other bonus or pile of bonuses-- but I

to a list who hadn't been mailed in a while, normally I would not say, "it's me, so and so"):
Subject Line: The only diet that works even when you "cheat"
Hi it's
I 	hope you're doing well!
I wanted to let you know I'm experimenting with some new
diet info I've been learning from a world-class fitness expert and
some professional athletes.
The things they are teaching me are incredible!
They make traditional "dieting" obsolete.
(HINT: 	You can "cheat" AND lose fat at the same time.)
And I'll tell you more over the next few weeks.
I 	know you will love it -- especially if you are someone who cannot
lose weight 	(especially in your belly!) no matter how hard you are
trying to now.
I'll send you more specific tips about this shortly.
Talk to you again soon!
-SIGNATURE
Okey dokey, a bit of explanation:
Unlike most emails, I did not have a call to action in this. And the reason why is we
simply didn't have the squeeze page ready for the sublist at this point, and I was eager to
get started. Plus, this list needed to be "warmed up" a bit. Many of the people on it had
not heard from us in weeks and, in some cases, months. So this was just a warm up email,
normally I would have a link to a squeeze page. But in this case they were cold and so not
only did I do one... but TWO... warm up emails. If you want you can do the same thing
just to perk up interest a bit before you put a squeeze page up and start building your

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
See how this email teases? And how it's designed from the get-go to generate interest
using curiosity and hanging out in their "world"? A world where you don't have to starve
yourself to lose weight, can "cheat", and still lose size? Of course, the product really does
deliver on this (which is a big help writing emails or selling anything online -- a great
product will do most of the "heavy lifting" for you, it's one of the big secrets of writing
money-making emails). These emails are all about the fanta5Y -- showing them a fantasy
they did not believe was possible before. And it's your job to show them it IS
possible. Okay, that's it for the warm up emails. Now we get down to business.
Remember, the two emails above COULD have sent them to a sublist, but we had to
warm them up first, since they hadn't been mailed in a while. Now here's the next one:

foods 	that make people gain a lot of fat. 	But the reality is, 	all
of the foods on the list are completely okay for most people to eat.
some of the foods 	included:

* Salty french fries
* Lasagna
* Cheese cake
* Cookies
* Chicago-style hot dogs
* Pizza
* Pastries
* Glazed doughnuts

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
This was the first email telling them to join the sublist. Or, as I liked to call it, "The
VIP" list. You can call it whatever you like-- the discount list, early bird discount list, etc.
The point is, they are now being told if they want the product at a discount, to join that
list. This does not mean you won't mail your main list, too. But this let's you get a head
count of how many people are interested in your product. If little or no people join your
sublist, then there's a good chance there's not a lot of demand for your product. Or, it
might mean you need to work harder on your emails. But I like to use it to measure
demand at a glance. Plus, as you will see later, this sublist is mailed differently. Finally,
notice how we're giving the fantasy here (and yes, it's all 100% true). Very important
when launching-- you have to give the fantasy but also make it believable (thus, the parts
about the diet being created by professional athletes and perfected by a world class fitness
expert). Okay, on to the next email (they are all daily now, except weekends):
Subject line: 	$3 tummy tuck?
Hello!
A lot of women get dangerous 	"tummy tuck" work done.
I would not recommend it, 	though.
For one thing, 	there are a lot of hidden dangers to getting tummy
tuck surgery -- including blood clots in your legs & lungs,
respiratory problems, 	heart complications and, 	in some rare cases,
even death!
Plus ••• 	they're unnecessary.
You don't need it.
There are natural "tummy tucks" that work just fine.
In fact, 	the new book I'm working on called 	shows
you a special kind of tummy tuck you can do 1-3 days per week that
will only cost you about $2-3 a day.
It also lets you eat your favorite bad foods, 	too.
Can't do that with a regular tummy tuck.
Sure, 	you can get the surgery.

Notice how this email (and all of them, really) is not long? They are mostly under
300 words and the point is just to kind of drop some tease and get out of Dodge -- tease
and get out, tease and get out ... all the while trying to persuade them to jump on the
discount list. Repetition is key here ... the longer you can draw this out the better (for this
"Email Players" newsletter launch, it took exactly 2 months from first email to launch,
that's a good time frame, but not always possible -- with this weight loss product we only
had a couple weeks, so you do what you can with the time you have ...) Also, the fantasy is
still being spelled out. I actually swiped the subject line from a magazine cover (I forget
which one). I love magazine cover headlines for using as subject line templates. If you
want some great idea-starters check out Magazines.com and look at the covers of Cosmo,
Mens Health and other magazines with lots of teasers. Even if you're not in the same
market, the way they structure those headlines makes for great subject line "fodder."
Finally, this email was an "invalidation" email-- where it invalidates a competing option
to our book (in this case, surgery). As we saw in last months' "Email Players" issue,
mocking the competition is a great email technique you can go to town with
(you could do an email every day just invalidating one of your competitors as described in
last month's issue). OK, let's move on to the next email. And by the way, there were
something like 14 or 15 of these pre-launch emails. I'm not going to display all of them
simply because this would become a 50 page newsletter and be impractical. So instead I'll
show a few more, then the launch emails. This is designed to be very simple, and it's
important to keep it simple the whole time. In fact, basically what you're doing is just
using the email methods you got in your free "Email Players Cookbook" and are learning
in these issues to write emails like you normally would ... except instead of selling
something, you're teasing them on to a sublist. Okay, onward...
-8-

Subject line: What really keeps women fat
Hil
Weight problems aren't usually "food" problems.
Or exercise problems.
Or even health problems.
They are usually **procrastination** problems.
It's true -- procrastination may not have caused you to gain your
extra weight, 	but it IS what is most likely keeping that extra
weight on and making sure it doesn't go anywhere.
A lot of us 	"plan" to do something about it.
We even get excited by the idea of doing something about it.
But in this society and time in history -- where temptation to eat
is everywhere and where almost all foods 	are loaded with sugar
(even "health" foods!) 	it's hard to really get started and stick
with anything.
I 	have seen this over and over.
It makes us emotional wrecks too!
Well, 	I 	think I 	have a solution to this.
My new book _____ is a blueprint for a VERY simple diet
plan that is easy to start, 	easy to stick with and easy to lose
inches on 	(it's not unusual to lose up to 20, 	maybe even 30, pounds
in your first month on it, 	while still eating your favorite 	"bad"
foods.)
It's PERFECT for getting past procrastination.
After all, 	you aren't depriving yourself.
You still get to eat yummy foods!
The book is being edited now, 	and will be ready soon.
To get it for 50% off during the release date, 	just go to this web

Notice how none of these emails are really "teaching" anything? I'm just having a
discussion with them in their world, about their problems and challenges. I'm not trying
to sell them, I'm trying to bond with them. I'm wanting them to know I CARE about
them, and that I have a solution to their problem. If you don't empathize with your
market, you're going to struggle writing emails --especially during a launch. People know
when you're just going through the motions. Luckily for me there are people in this
market I'm close to and so I know all about their pains and frustrations. That's what you
want to do -- talk to them about their pains and frustrations, show them the fantasy of
what it will be like if they use your product, and give them real, lasting hope that things
can get better and that you're not some scum bag who is going to rip them off. If there's a
"secret" to email it's bonding with people just like a talk show host does. Think Oprah.
She's her audience's best friend because she gets down from the billionaire media diva
soap box and instead becomes one with them. It's perfect email strateg:x, too. So in
this case, we start with a problem/frustration, then lead into the solution. You can do this
over and over if you want. Make sure you use the 30-day game plan in the "Email Players
Cookbook" too (you could do a successful launch just using those emails formats).
Subject line: Doctors refusing to treat overweight patients
Hello!
Unfortunately, 	this email's subject line is true.
A couple months ago, 	I 	read a news article from the South Florida
Sun-Sentinel saying how a growing number of doctors are setting
weight limits for new patients. 	Some of the doctors say the main
reason is their exam tables and other equipment can't handle people
over a certain weight, 	plus heavy women run a higher risk of
complications.
Scary isn't it?
If this trend continues it could be disastrous.
-10-

If memory serves, this email drove a lot of people to the sublist. Why? Because it
has a LOT of interesting things going for it like ( 1) News appeal (that was a real news
story, are you reading news stories that affect your market. .. ?) (2) Bonding (who else is
telling their lists about this sort of thing in the weight loss world?) (3) Conversation (this is
not a sales pitch --it's a conversation, yet it does sell them on doing something). Best part?
This email is VERY easy for you to model. just find a news story that affects your market
in some way and use the above as a template. This is great for launching a product or a
sale, but you can use this for any kind of regular email you send out. Okay, let's do one
more pre-launch email and then we'll tackle the launch process. This is the email that
went out the day before the launch began:
Subject line: 	Diets that torment you
Hi,
Most diets torment you.
You know it as well as 	I 	do.
Take this one diet I was looking at last year.

OK, again, very easy to model this if you want. Invalidate your competition,
MOCK them even (yes, MOCK your competition like this one does, just refrain from
naming names... ). Show them the fantasy of what you got and how they can get it a
discount. It really is this easy. And again, the more time you have for this the better. In our
case, I think we had 2.5 weeks, so there were more emails, but these were some of the

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
ones that pulled the most new subscribers on the sublist. Now for the actual launch. It
lasted from Thursday to midnight on Sunday. The main list got one email for Thursday,
another on Friday and another on Saturday, then two on Sunday. Sometimes I send more
than two on Sunday -- as many as three or four, just depends -- but in this case I sent two.
I'll show you all 5 emails so you can see how simple this is. Then, I'll show you the emails
that were sent to the sublist, which were a bit different (and in greater numbers). But first,
here is the first email to announce the product is ready on that Thursday:
Subject line: 	Download --
Hit
is now ready!
Originally, 	I was going to let early bird buyers have it for 50%
off. But I 	changed my mind. 	I want to give you an even BIGGER
discount. 	So if you buy it before sunday at midnight you can have
it for 64% off 	(for even more savings!)
You can download it here:
(LINK)
SIGNATURE
P.S. 	Since not everyone gets their emails due to spam filters, 	etc,
I'll be sending short reminders about this tomorrow, 	Saturday and
Sunday, 	too.
Remember, 	the discount ends sunday night:
(LINK)
Pretty simple, isn't it? That's because you do not need to make it fancy or long or
drawn out. If you did the pre-launch emails correctly (and again you can use all the ones
you've seen here as models, they're very simple) then they are WAITING for this email.
Also, a couple more things: (1) I purposely used the word "download" in the subject line
because many times (although this is not a hard and fast rule) the word "download" helps
increase open rates. Not all the time, of course. But why not put your best foot forward?
You can also test different subject lines during launches, which if we hadn't been so lazy
we would have done. But if you have a list of over 10,000 (we didn't) I would recommend
doing a quick split test (for sales, as well as open rates) before rolling out to the main list

on this first email and, even, on the rest of them if you have the time and energy. And
secondly, notice how we told them they're getting an even bigger discount than intended?
The September "Email Players" issue went into detail on why we did that (it was half an
accident and half strategic) and "ethically fibbed." So if you want a refresher on that, just
pull out the September issue and you're golden. Okay, on to the next email they got on
Friday:
Subject line: 	2 more days for 	discount
Hello!
Just a reminder:
______ is now ready. And if you buy it before Sunday at
midnight, 	you can have it for 64% off 	(originally it was only going
to be 50% off, 	but I 	like to reward early action-takers!)
Here is where to get it for 64% off:
(URL)
SIGNATURE
Another short, sweet and to-the-point email. Again, no need for a long, droning
email. You CAN do it if you want (there is no right or wrong way for any of this -- do
whatever works). But in our case I just wanted to remind them. Incidentally, when you do
launches this way I'm showing you what often happens is, you get almost all of your sales
on the first and last days of the launch. The two middle days (Friday and Saturday)
usually yield just a trickle of sales. So don't panic if on the middle days you don't see a
huge explosion of activity. That's normal. Most people by this stage arc either hot to buy
and respond on day one or are total procrastinators and put it off until the last minute.
Here's Saturday's email (email #3 to the main list):
Subject line: Last day for __ discount
Hi!
The 64% off 	discount ends tomorrow.
-----
If you buy it before the deadline, 	you can have the book for 64%
off what it normally costs on Amazon and other major online
bookstores. 	Go here today to get it for 64% off while you still can:

A 3rd short and to-the-point email. Again, you don't always have to do this. In
fact, often times to my BenSettle.com list I do longer more informational emails to my
main list during the launch (since that is what they are used to getting from me and it is a
different kind of list than this weight loss list, with different expectations). But for most
products/lists what I'm showing you here is fine. Nothing real fancy about it in this case
either. Finally, the last two Sunday emails -- one went out in the morning, the other before
the deadline. In hindsight I should have sent more emails out to them that day, maybe one
or two more that were more informational (like mini articles). But it's okay; these still got
the job done. The first one went out at 12:01 am Sunday morning and the second one
went out at 11 :46 pm that night (right before the deadline):

-- 15 of 27 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
Also, 	one more thing:
I 	realize some people are reading this on their smart phones.
And maybe you're stuck in an airport or somewhere where you cannot
get a secure connection to the Internet. 	If that is the case, 	just
email me at ______ in the next few minutes 	(before midnight) 	and I'll send you
a special link to order from when you get back to your computer.
I 	hope to hear from you soon!
SIGNATURE
Not a whole lot to explain on these last two. The timing is important, though.
They wake up and see and email and then again are reminded right towards the end.
There's an almost "eBay like" effect when you do this that'll be much more obvious when
I show you the sublist emails next. Also, notice the last email -- this is for people who are
reading it on their phones and cannot easily order. I like to give people a chance to buy by
sending me an email like that. It often yields a few m.ore sales and you're doing
them. a service at the sam.e tim.e (you might be surprised how many people are stuck
in traffic or at an airport, etc-- it happens). Finally, I keep saying this, but in hindsight I
should have sent a couple more out that day that were more informational and interesting
and not just straight announcements. But either way, these emails got the job done. And
you can model your launch emails exactly how they are here if you choose. If you do the
pre-launch emails correctly, these will be more than enough to do the deed.
Okay, next up is the sublist emails. These are slightly different and more frequent.
That is because you're mailing specifically to people who raised their hand and said they
were interested and wanted to be alerted when your launch started. These are your
HOTTEST prospects. No, they won't all buy. But a lot of them will if you did the pre-
launch phase correctly (and realize many of these people have also been getting your
main list emails). So instead of 5 emails over the weekend, they got I 0 emails (most of
them on Sunday). Plus, one of the I 0 is just a "it's coming tomorrow" email just to get
them ready and remind them ahead of time. Here is that first "reminder" email that went
out that Wednesday:
Subject line: ____ discount tomorrow
Hello,
-16-

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
Just a heads up:
I'm launching my new ______ book tomorrow. 	Since you signed up
to be notified when it's ready 	(so you can get a 50% discount) 	I'll
be sending frequent reminders throughout the weekend.
Talk to you tomorrowt
SIGNATURE
No bells & whistles, just a simple reminder. Pretty self explanatory, but what is
worth noting is I just wanted to mail the sublist before the launch and remind them, but
also warn them they'll be getting frequent reminders throughout the weekend. By
reminding them you're serving them (many people appreciate it-- they know theire
procrastinators). And also, you're respecting them and their time. You're telling
them what to expect and that's always good when you're about to bombard them with
emails (I figure it's just common courtesy). Now, for the first day of the launch (that
Thursday) to the sublist:
Subject line: The 	is ready
Hi!
is now for sale.
You can get it for 64% off 	(I decided to give you a 64% discount
instead of 	just a 50% discount) 	at this link until Sunday at
midnight:
(URL)
SIGNATURE
P.S. Since you specifically asked to be notified about this sale •••
and since a lot of people don't get their emails delivered anymore,
I'm going to send frequent reminders throughout the weekend,
especially on Sunday.
Remember, 	the discount ends sunday night:
(URL)
Notice the subject line is slightly different than the main list email for day I? That's

because I wanted to switch it up a bit -- they already saw that since they are also on the
main list. And I don't want them to get numb to the same verbiage. Plus, I sent this (and
the other sublist emails) out at different times. You don't want people to get all these
emails at the same times. Instead, the main list got it at 12 am, and this first sublist email
went out at 5 am. These are litde things, but they can add up to big impact over time.
Here is the next day (Friday) email:
Subject line: -- discount ends in 2 days
Hello!
The __ is now ready.
It's the only diet I've ever seen that lets you safely, 	simply and
realistically lose fat while still eating your favorite bad foods.
It's based on pure science and works even for women with stubborn
fat that refuses to go away.
Here is where to get it for 64% off:
(URL)

This was just a reminder email. Again, a similar but slighdy altered subject line
from the main list Friday email. Nothing fancy --just a brief email showing them where
to go to get the discount with a few benefits thrown in for good measure. Remember,
these two days (Friday and Saturday) usually don't yield a whole lot of sales so don't panic
if they just trickle in. Thursday and Sunday tend to get the most sales (at least, that's been
my experience). Here is the Saturday email (day #3):
Subject line: Last day for 	discount
Hi!
The 	discount ends tomorrow.
Order before the deadline and it's yours for a full 64% off.
Details at:
(URL)

-- 18 of 27 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
SIGNATURE
Notice again, these are not long, drawn-out emails. I could (and probably should,
in hindsight) have thrown one or two informational type emails in. But it's not necessary if
you do the pre-launch emails correctly. Also on the Saturday emails (both for the main list
and sub list) I like to use the "last day" verbiage even though it's not technically the last
day. It gives the feeling of urgency but in the body it talks about how tomorrow is the last
day (so you're not lying to them).Just a small, subtle thing to keep in mind. Next up is the
Sunday emails. This is where the real POWER of the launch comes in. This is why you
have a sublist. And this is where you will likely make the most sales (or break even on the
same number of sales you made Thursday on day one). This is where you create a count
down effect -- sort of like how eBay does it -- that builds tension, motivates the
procrastinators and even persuades people to buy who normally wouldn't have.
To my knowledge tl1is count down method was created by Michael Sen off even though
you see lots of goo-roos doing it now all the sudden (and pretending like they created it in
their infinite jeenius... ). But this is a Senoff original and I give him credit for it. I'm going
to post them all here without commentary until the last email. They are self explanatory
and very simple (where people go wrong in email is trying to complicate things):
12:01 am

641 discount ends today.
Go here to get it for 641 off:
(URL)

Hi!
The clock is ticking on the 	sale.

Sec? Simple! And that's the whole point. You can model these emails exactly how I
did them if you want Gust insert your benefits, credibility elements, etc in where
necessary). You can also play around with them however you want using whatever
verbiage you want. What's important is the structure more than the actual wording.
Besides that, here are some other things to note:
• 	Timing: I timed these sublist emails to go out throughout the day, but I
also timed them to go out at times when the main list was not getting
them. They were getting both the main list and sublist emails and this
helps keep that urgency going, and feels like less of a bombardment than
if they got both sublist and main list emails at the exact same time
• 	Subject line: Don't think you have to use my exact subject lines -- I've
never tested any of them and, even if I had, they would not necessarily
be best for your list. These are just samples, feel free to play around with
them as you see fit. Same goes for the body copy. Remember, the timed
structure and frequency is what's most important, not the words
-22-
www.BcnSettle.com 	• 	Copyright 20 11 Ben Settle, All Rights Reserved 	• 	Published By Settle, ILC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

THE 	EMAIL PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
• 	Varied up: Notice I don't hit them with the exact same verbiage every
time -- I do use the same benefits and credibility elements, but not
worded the exact same way. Keep it varied up and you'll keep people
more interested
• 	Countdown: The timed "eBay-like" structure cannot be overstated
here -- it's what makes this sale work
• 	Relentless follow up: Yes, you are being relendess, but don't think
people do not appreciate it -- they do! I've gotten emails many times
where, at the LAST hour, someone says, "Ben thank you for reminding
me, I didn't see this until just now!" Not everyone checks their emails 10
times per day, and not everyone necessarily gets all your emails. Will you
annoy a few people? Maybe, maybe not. That's what the delete button
and "unsubscribe" link is for
• 	Smart phone PS: Don't forget to add the smart phone component for
those who are not at their computers or cannot get to their computer
and are reading on their smart phones. You won't get a ton more sales
doing this, but you will likely get enough to be worth the effort. Gust
create a sales link and send it to them -- make sure you give them a
deadline to order by, though ... )
And that's all there is to it.
This may seem like a lot of moving parts but really, it's not. just use my
methodology above "as is" (feel free to model and adapt the emails as you see fit, although
if you're in the weight loss niche do NOT copy verbatim -- use your own words, etc). Yes,
it's simple. And that's the whole point -- simplicity. If this was complicated I would
not do it, nor be able to KEEP doing it over and over. In fact, I use this exact
same methodology not just for product launches, but also for special sales of any kind of
product. You can also simply take one of your products down for a few weeks and retool/
improve it and then re-launch it using this methodology (a good offer is, "when it comes
back the price goes up, but if you get it during the weekend I re-launch it you can get it at
its original price... " I've done that many times).

we have a saying on our team here at
Basketball Renegades: 	(Good opener, 	too, 	I 	likey)
"Follow those who seek the truth. 	Run
from those who claim to have found it."
I 	don't claim to have all the answers. 	(Personally, 	I would start with this
sentence, 	but I'm kinda paranoid about cutting everything that doesn't
absolutely 	need to be included, 	the opener and quote above are great, 	but
you have to ask yourself are they essential? 	If they are, 	keep 	'em, 	but if
not, 	you might want to start with "I don't claim to have all the answers'
which in itself is not a bad· opener either)

honest, 	and tell you about the early
struggle as well.

career, 	and I was determined to achieve them
at all costs.
I 	used to train for hours and hours every day -
